title: Spinal cord stimulation in 60 cases of intractable pain
journal_name: Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery, and Psychiatry
study_question: What outcomes occur after spinal cord stimulation for intractable pain?
population_assessed: 60 patients with intractable pain receiving spinal cord stimulators
outcome_measures: Significant benefit, modest benefit, no effect, made worse
inclusion_criteria: Intractable pain patients receiving spinal cord stimulators at one neurosurgery department
answer_to_study_question: 70% benefited; 46.7% significant, 23.3% modest, 20% no effect, 6.7% worse.
